symptom: a hard hit to the thigh and a bruise that never faded and a firm lump weeks later
hook: a shell of new bone was forming inside the thigh muscle with a hard white rim around a softer center
whatYouSee: A dense shell of new bone forming in the soft tissue of the thigh with a rim of solid white bone around a softer center.
whyItMatters: A deep bruise triggered the muscle to lay down real bone tissue in a place bone was never meant to be.
treatment: Rest and gentle physio while it settles and surgery only later if a mature lump keeps getting in the way.
takeaway: A bruise can heal itself right into bone.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a hard hit to the thigh and a bruise that never faded and a firm lump weeks later.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And a shell of new bone was forming inside the thigh muscle with a hard white rim around a softer center.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Myositis ossificans

👀 What you see:
A dense shell of new bone forming in the soft tissue of the thigh with a rim of solid white bone around a softer center.

🦴 Why it matters:
A deep bruise triggered the muscle to lay down real bone tissue in a place bone was never meant to be.

💊 Treatment:

Rest and gentle physio while it settles and surgery only later if a mature lump keeps getting in the way.
